title: Plans Launched for 100-Mile Hydrogen Pipeline into Nottinghamshire
content: Cadent has launched plans for a major new hydrogen pipeline that will supply low carbon hydrogen to industry across the Humber, Lincolnshire and Nottinghamshire region, supporting manufacturers of steel, chemicals, bricks, food and more with a cleaner alternative to natural gas while boosting local jobs, encouraging inward investment and enabling a route to market for hydrogen producers to help decarbonise this industrial heartland.  Mayor of the East Midlands Claire Ward said the H2East Pipeline represents ambitious investment at the heart of the East Midlands Growth Plan, with green growth driving innovation, attracting new investment and creating sustainable economic foundations for generations to come by connecting hydrogen production at HyMarnham Energy Park with major regional employers.  The pipeline will run approximately 150 kilometres from Immingham to Newark, will be largely underground and is in early development stages, with Cadent set to consult people on route proposals later this year as the project enters the Governmentâ€™s Development Consent Order planning process expected to take four to five years
author: Analysts Account
